etymology.description: Original: semantic 爪(爪) (zhǎo, 'hands') + phonetic 角(角) (jiǎo, 'horn') + semantic 女(女) (nǚ, 'woman') -> Meaning uncertain -> Later: 母(母) (mǔ, 'mother') + 中(中) (zhōng, 'middle') + 女(女) (nǚ, 'daughter') -> ideogram
etymology.interpretation: The character 婁(lóu) originally combined elements representing hands, a horn for phonetic value, and a woman, suggesting a complex or uncertain meaning. It later evolved into a combination of mother, central, and daughter elements, further abstracting its meaning.
